title: Soil Life Atlas Pipeline — Submission Form
description: Form schema for generating a `nextflow run soillifeatlas/nf-core-soillifeatlas` command. Parameter names here map 1:1 to the real Nextflow params declared in nextflow.config / nextflow_schema.json of the v0.1.0-dev pipeline. The website renders this schema into a multi-step form; the `command_generator.js` module turns the validated form state into a ready-to-copy CLI string.

upload.title: Upload data
upload.description: Your per-sample feature-intensity matrix, MS2 spectra, and (optional) treatment metadata. v0.1 uses explicit file paths rather than a samplesheet; samplesheet-driven runs land in v0.2.

soil_intensity.title: Soil intensity matrix
soil_intensity.description: Per-sample feature-intensity matrix from MZmine (one feature per row, one sample per column). Parquet preferred; CSV accepted. Feature IDs must match the MGF scan IDs.

soil_mgf.title: Soil MS2 spectra (MGF)
soil_mgf.description: GNPS-style MGF with consensus MS2 spectra per feature. Scan IDs in the MGF must correspond to feature IDs in the intensity matrix.

sample_metadata.title: Sample metadata
sample_metadata.description: Optional. TSV or CSV with one row per sample and a `sample_id` column. Treatment columns (e.g. drought, climate) enable the treatment-effects readout. Without it, the pipeline still returns the composition table but skips EVAL_TREATMENT.

instrument.title: Instrument & atlas
instrument.description: Instrument mode and reference-atlas choice. Defaults reproduce Samrat et al. 2025.

ion_mode.title: Ion mode
ion_mode.description: Only positive mode is supported in v0.1. NEG-mode processing is blocked by missing soil IS coverage; lands in v0.2.

is_compound.title: Internal standard reference compound
is_compound.description: Deuterated lipid used for L2 IS normalization. LPE_18d7 is the Samrat 2025 default (primary adduct [M+H]+, m/z 487.3525). The 13 EquiSPLASH standards are listed for forward compatibility; only LPE_18d7 is validated for v0.1.

is_spiked_pmol.title: Internal standard spike (pmol)
is_spiked_pmol.description: Amount of the internal standard spiked into each sample before extraction. 100 pmol is the Samrat 2025 default.

atlas_version.title: Atlas version
atlas_version.description: Reference atlas release to decompose against. v0.1.0 is the only published atlas; additional versions appear as new Zenodo deposits land.

advanced.title: Advanced (optional)
advanced.description: Scientific-constant overrides. Defaults reproduce the iter3a_floor020 configuration from Samrat 2025. Changes here break the pinned-atlas SHA256 guarantee, so keep defaults unless you know why you're changing them.

rie_floor.title: RIE floor
rie_floor.description: Lower bound on the RIE (Response/Ionization Efficiency) correction multiplier. Floor = 0.20 is the iter3a default and the critical fix for the original RIE over-amplification bug; at floor < 0.10 low-intensity features dominate the composition vector.

restrict_archaea_to_archlips.title: Restrict Archaea to ArchLips reference features
restrict_archaea_to_archlips.description: L5 correction: for the Archaea kingdom, only keep features that match the ArchLips-validated reference set. This is the Samrat 2025 reference-quality filter and is on by default.

decomposition_methods.title: Decomposition methods
decomposition_methods.description: Which decomposition methods to run. All 4 run in parallel by default; drop some to shave runtime. The primary method (selected below) must be in this list.

primary_method.title: Primary decomposition method
primary_method.description: Method reported as THE composition in the final report; others are kept as sensitivity checks. fc_weighted_bc is the Samrat 2025 best performer (BC = 0.131 vs expected on ClimGrass).

skip_gnps.title: Skip GNPS2 networking call
skip_gnps.description: Use matchms local fallback instead of the GNPS2 REST API for molecular networking. Faster and offline-friendly. Off by default.

skip_annotate.title: Skip annotation subworkflow
skip_annotate.description: The ANNOTATE subworkflow (MS-DIAL, SIRIUS, diag-ion, ArchLips) runs parallel enrichment and is NOT on the decomposition critical path. On by default (demo behavior).
